devanagari: वानप्रस्थ संन्यासाश्रमधर्म निरूपणम् । श्रीभगवानुवाच । वनं विविक्षुः पुत्रेषु भार्यां न्यस्य सहैव वा । वन एव वसेत् शान्तः तृतीयं भागमायु...
english_devnagari: vānaprastha saṃnyāsāśramadharma nirūpaṇam śrībhagavānuvāca vanaṃ vivikṣuḥ putreṣu bhāryāṃ nyasya sahaiva vā vana eva vaset śāntaḥ tṛtīyaṃ bhāgamāyuṣaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation:
The glorious Lord continued:
A person desirous of entering the Vānaprastha (the third stage of) life should either entrust his wife to the care of his sons or take her with him. He should spend the third quarter of his life (upto the age of seventy-five years) in a forest.

devanagari: कन्दमूलफलैः वन्यैः मेध्यैः वृत्तिं प्रकल्पयेत् । वसीत वल्कलं वासः तृणपर्णाजिनानि च ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kandamūlaphalaiḥ vanyaiḥ medhyaiḥ vṛttiṃ prakalpayet vasīta valkalaṃ vāsaḥ tṛṇaparṇājināni ca
translation: He should live on bulbous and other roots, fruits which are regarded as pure and which grow in forests. For clothing he should wear bark-garments, mats of grass and leaves and deer-skins.

devanagari: केशरोमनखश्मश्रुमलानि बिभृयाद् दतः । न धावेदप्सु मज्जेत त्रिकालं स्थण्डिलेशयः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: keśaromanakhaśmaśrumalāni bibhṛyād dataḥ na dhāvedapsu majjeta trikālaṃ sthaṇḍileśayaḥ
translation: He should allow to grow the hair on his head, beard, nails and dirt (dust) on his body. He should not brush his teeth. He should dip his body in water (like a pestle, without cleansing his body) three times (morning, noon and evening) a day and sleep on bare ground (without using even grass or leaves to cover it).

devanagari: ग्रीष्मे तप्येत पञ्चाग्नीन् वर्षास्वासारषाड् जले । आकण्ठमग्नः शिशिरे एवंवृत्तस्तपश्चरेत् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: grīṣme tapyeta pañcāgnīn varṣāsvāsāraṣāḍ jale ākaṇṭhamagnaḥ śiśire evaṃvṛttastapaścaret

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative middle third single], [optative passive third single]

translation: In summer, he should perform (what is technically known as) Pañcāgni sādhana i.e. exposure of the body to the heat of fire from four quarters while the fifth fire, viz. the sun is scorching from the sky. In the rainy-season, he should observe the vow of Abhrāvakāśa i.e. drenching the body in heavy showers and in the winter he should stay immersed in the water upto the neck (and observe the udaka-vāsa vrata). He should perform penance following the above way of life.

devanagari: अग्निपक्वं समश्नीयात् कालपक्वमथापि वा । उलूखलाश्मकुट्टो वा दन्तोलूखल एव वा ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: agnipakvaṃ samaśnīyāt kālapakvamathāpi vā ulūkhalāśmakuṭṭo vā dantolūkhala eva vā
translation: He may eat what is baked on the fire or fruits, etc. ripened in course of time. He may subsist on what is pounded in a mortar or crushed with a stone or powdered with his teeth as mortar and pestle.

devanagari: स्वयं सञ्चिनुयात् सर्वम् आत्मनो वृत्तिकारणम् । देशकालबलाभिज्ञो नाददीतान्यदाऽहृतम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: svayaṃ sañcinuyāt sarvam ātmano vṛttikāraṇam deśakālabalābhijño nādadītānyadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: He should, with his own effort, procure whatever is required for his subsistence. Though he has full knowledge of his environments, of the season, and of his own capacity, he should not use it for storing (food, fruits, etc.) for some other occasion.[1] Nor should he accept what is offered by others.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
labdhe nave nave'nnādye purāṇaṃ tu parityajet /—BH.P.VII. 12. 19.

devanagari: वन्यैश्चरुपुरोडाशैः निर्वपेत् कालचोदितान् । न तु श्रौतेन पशुना मां यजेत वनाश्रमी ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vanyaiścarupuroḍāśaiḥ nirvapet kālacoditān na tu śrautena paśunā māṃ yajeta vanāśramī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: He should perform the prescribed periodic sacrificial duties with oblations of the forest produce and Puroḍāśa (prepared out of them). But under no circumstances should a Vānaprastha worship me with animal sacrifices as laid, down in the Śruti texts.

devanagari: अग्निहोत्रं च दर्शश्च पौर्णमासश्च पूर्ववत् । चातुर्मास्यानि च मुनेः आम्नातानि च नैगमैः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: agnihotraṃ ca darśaśca paurṇamāsaśca pūrvavat cāturmāsyāni ca muneḥ āmnātāni ca naigamaiḥ
translation: Maintenance of sacred fire and regular offering of oblations to it (Agnihotra), performance of half-monthly sacrifices on the New Moon day (Darśa) and the Full Moon Day (Pūrṇamāsa) and the seasonal sacrifices performed at the beginning of the months of Kārttika, Phālguna and Āṣāḍha (which mark the beginnings of the main seasons in India), have been prescribed for a Vānaprastha by Vedic teachers.

devanagari: एवं चीर्णेन तपसा मुनिः र्धमनिसंततः । मां तपोमयमाराध्य ऋषिलोकादुपैति माम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ cīrṇena tapasā muniḥ rdhamanisaṃtataḥ māṃ tapomayamārādhya ṛṣilokādupaiti mām
translation: The sage who becomes spread over by a web of (protruding) veins (i.e. is reduced to a skeleton for want of flesh) by performing penance as described above, propitiates me who am the embodiment of Tapas (austerities) and comes- to me passing through the region of sages (via Mahar-loka, etc.)

devanagari: यस्त्वेतत्कृच्छ्रतश्चीर्णं तपो निःश्रेयसं महत् । कामायाल्पीयसे युञ्ज्याद् बालिशः कोऽपरस्ततः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yastvetatkṛcchrataścīrṇaṃ tapo niḥśreyasaṃ mahat kāmāyālpīyase yuñjyād bāliśaḥ ko
translation: Who else can be more foolish than the person who dissipates for fulfilling flimsy desires such as. great penance performed with enormous troubles and capable of securing him Mokṣa?

devanagari: यदासौ नियमेऽकल्पो जरया जातवेपथुः । आत्मन्यग्नीन् समारोप्य मच्चित्तोऽग्निं समाविशेत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yadāsau niyame ātmanyagnīn samāropya maccitto
translation: [2] When, shaking with decrepitude, he becomes incapable of observing the duties prescribed (for the Vānaprastha stage), he should withdraw within himself the three sacred fires (viz. Āhavanīya, Gārhapatya and Dakṣiṇa which he is maintaining for Agnihotra) and concentrating his mind on me, he should enter fire.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: A Vānaprastha who continues to perform penance to the end of his life attains Mokṣa. If at the end of the Vānaprastha period, renunciation is generated in his mind (even dimly), he is eligible to become a Sannyāsin. But incase of premature incapatiation (if he becomes incapable of observing his vows before the end of that stage), the above self consignment to fire is recommended.

devanagari: यदा कर्मविपाकेषु लोकेषु निरयात्मसु । विरागो जायते सम्यङ् न्यस्ताग्निः प्रव्रजेत्ततः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā karmavipākeṣu lokeṣu nirayātmasu virāgo jāyate samyaṅ nyastāgniḥ pravrajettataḥ
translation: When a genuine feeling of renunciation is generated towards the worlds or to even higher blissful regions obtained by merits) as no better than the hell (full of tortures and miseries), he should withdraw (as per procedure laid down in śastras) all the (three) fires within himself and enter the order of sannyāsins.

devanagari: इष्ट्वा यथोपदेशं मां दत्त्वा सर्वस्वमृत्विजे । अग्नीन् स्वप्राण आवेश्य निरपेक्षः परिव्रजेत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iṣṭvā yathopadeśaṃ māṃ dattvā sarvasvamṛtvije agnīn svaprāṇa āveśya nirapekṣaḥ parivrajet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect middle first single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having worshipped me as per Śāstric instructions,[3] and having gifted everything what one has to the sacrificial priest, one should deposit the three sacred fires within his own Inner Self (antaryāmin) and he should enter the order of sannyāsa completely free from any longings.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
A Vānaprastha should perform the Prājāpatya sacrifice and also eight śrāddhas before becoming a Sannyāsin—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: विप्रस्य वै संन्यसतो देवा दारादिरूपिणः । विघ्नान् कुर्वन्त्ययं ह्यस्मान् आक्रम्य समियात् परम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: viprasya vai saṃnyasato devā dārādirūpiṇaḥ vighnān kurvantyayaṃ hyasmān ākramya samiyāt param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

[nominative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: In the way of a Brāhmaṇa who is bent on renouncing the world as a sannyāsin, gods create impediments in the for in of his wife and others (for dissuading him from sannyāsa), as they are afraid that he would supersede them, and attain Brahman.

devanagari: बिभृयाच्चेन् मुनिर्वासः कौपीनाच्छादनं परम् । त्यक्तं न दण्डपात्राभ्याम् अन्यत् किञ्चिद् अनापदि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: bibhṛyāccen munirvāsaḥ kaupīnācchādanaṃ param tyaktaṃ na daṇḍapātrābhyām anyat kiñcid anāpadi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: If a sage entering the order of sannyāsa requires any clothing other than his loin-cloth (kaupīna), he should take only that much as is sufficient to cover his private parts. Except in case of emergency or critical plight (such as serious illness), he should not have anything that he has renounced (at the time of becoming a sannyāsin) with the exception of a staff (symbolic of his being a sannyāsin and a water-jar (made of gourd).

devanagari: दृष्टिपूतं न्यसेत् पादं वस्त्रपूतं पिबेत् जलम् । सत्यपूतां वदेद् वाचं मनःपूतं समाचरेत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭipūtaṃ nyaset pādaṃ vastrapūtaṃ pibet jalam satyapūtāṃ vaded vācaṃ manaḥpūtaṃ samācaret
translation: A sannyāsin should place his foot forward only when, the ground is purified by eyes (that is, it is scanned and found free from living beings and thus there would be no violence to life in stepping over that spot). He should drink water purifled by straining it with a piece of cloth. He should utter a word after convincing himself that what is expressed thereby is true. He should adopt a course of conduct according to the dictates of his conscience.

devanagari: मौनानीहानिलायामाः दण्डा वाक्देहचेतसाम् । न ह्येते यस्य सन्त्यङ्ग वेणुभिर्न भवेद् यतिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: maunānīhānilāyāmāḥ daṇḍā vākdehacetasām na hyete yasya santyaṅga veṇubhirna bhaved yatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: Silence,[4] renunciation of acts (for fulfilling one’s desires) and the control of breath, these are the internal controls of speech, body and mind. Dear Uddhava I He who does not have these controls, is no Sannyāsin as one does not make a real sannyāsin with a bamboo-staff.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
EK.: Repetition of God’s name of OṂ constitutes the control of speech. Breath control is the daṇḍa of the body and concentration in the Brahman is the control of the mind.—EB. 18.97-102.

devanagari: भिक्षां चतुर्षु वर्णेषु विगर्ह्यान् वर्जयन् चरेत् । सप्तागारान् असङ्क्लृप्तान् तुष्येत् लब्धेन तावता ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhikṣāṃ caturṣu varṇeṣu vigarhyān varjayan caret saptāgārān asaṅklṛptān tuṣyet labdhena tāvatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Avoiding the houses of the reproachful, one may go for alms to the houses of all the four Varṇas[5] (castes) among the Brāhmaṇas.[6] He should go to seven houses without giving pre-intimation and should be content with whatever one gets.[7]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
EK. clarifies: Before the advent of the Kali age, this was the rule. But in the Kali age, Brāhmaṇas follow different non-Brāhmaṇa professions and vocations. So we have all the four Varṇas in the Brāhmaṇa community itself. In the Kali age a sannyāsī should limit himself to the houses of Brāhmaṇas only. The exception of a reproachful Brāhmaṇa is of course binding.—EB. 11. 104-113.

Footnote [6]:
Kramasandarbha and VC. classify Brāhmaṇas as follows:
(1) Those who live on gifts (dāna) from others.
(2) Teachers of Vedas and Śāstras.
(3) Those who maintain themselves on fees received at sacrifices.
(4) Those who live upon food-grains gleaned from fields and market places.
Thus the mode of living (vṛttibheda) is the criterion in deciding the Varṇas among Brāhmaṇas.

Footnote [7]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā advises that a recluse should beg in the houses of Brahmin—Brāhmaṇas first and then Brahmin-Kṣattriyas, etc.

devanagari: बहिर्जलाशयं गत्वा तत्रोपस्पृश्य वाग्यतः । विभज्य पावितं शेषं भुञ्जीताशेषमाहृतम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: bahirjalāśayaṃ gatvā tatropaspṛśya vāgyataḥ vibhajya pāvitaṃ śeṣaṃ bhuñjītāśeṣamāhṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pū ], [accusative single from √ pū ]

translation: He should go to some reservoir of water (a lake, a river, etc.) outside the village or town. After sipping water (as Ācamana) in silence, he should sanctify the food (sprinkling it with a few drops of water charged with twelve Praṇavas, should divide that food in four parts (alotting them to Brahmā, Viṣṇu, the Sun-god and living beings and immersing Viṣṇu’s part into water and placing the one for living beings on the ground), he should finish the remaining holy food. (He should not store anything for the future).

devanagari: एकश्चरेत् महीमेतां निःसङ्गः संयतेन्द्रियः । आत्मक्रीड आत्मरत आत्मवान् समदर्शनः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ekaścaret mahīmetāṃ niḥsaṅgaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ ātmakrīḍa ātmarata ātmavān samadarśanaḥ
translation: With his senses and mind under control and perfectly unattached, he should wander all over the world without a companion, indulging in the recreation and bliss of his soul. Engrossed in his Self, he should view all with an equal eye.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: He should see that both sentients and non-sentients are a part of the body of the Lord.

devanagari: विविक्तक्षेमशरणो मद्‍भावविमलाशयः । आत्मानं चिन्तयेदेकमभेदेन मया मुनिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: viviktakṣemaśaraṇo mad‍bhāvavimalāśayaḥ ātmānaṃ cintayedekamabhedena mayā muniḥ
translation: Staying in a lonely yet safe place and with his mind purified by devotion to me, the sage should deeply meditate upon the one-ness of the self and its identity with me.[9]
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Padaratnāvalī: The sage should contemplate that the Lord is different from Jīva and that I am the Supreme Being established by the Veda
(...śrutyā mayā siddham ātmānam ekam mukhyaṃ cintayed abhedena jīvenaikyaṃ na cintayet)

devanagari: अन्वीक्षेतात्मनो बन्धं मोक्षं च ज्ञाननिष्ठया । बन्ध इन्द्रियविक्षेपो मोक्ष एषां च संयमः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: anvīkṣetātmano bandhaṃ mokṣaṃ ca jñānaniṣṭhayā bandha indriyavikṣepo mokṣa eṣāṃ ca saṃyamaḥ
translation: By application of his spiritual knowledge he should investigate into the nature of the bondage and liberation of the soul (and understand that) distraction of senses (to their objects of enjoyment) is the bondage and subjugation and control of senses is the Mokṣa.

devanagari: तस्मान्नियम्य षड्वर्गं मद्‍भावेन चरेन् मुनिः । विरक्तः क्षुद्रकामेभ्यो लब्ध्वा आत्मनि सुखं महत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmānniyamya ṣaḍvargaṃ mad‍bhāvena caren muniḥ viraktaḥ kṣudrakāmebhyo labdhvā ātmani sukhaṃ mahat
translation: Therefore, controlling the group of six enemies (i.e. passions like lust, anger, avarice, etc. or five senses plus the inner sense—the mind) the sage, should renounce trifling pleasures of senses, enjoying thereby the great bliss within his Self. While contemplating on me, the sage should carry on his pilgrimage on the earth.

devanagari: पुरग्रामव्रजान् सार्थान् भिक्षार्थं प्रविशंश्चरेत् । पुण्यदेश सरिच्छैशैल वनाश्रमवतीं महीम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: puragrāmavrajān sārthān bhikṣārthaṃ praviśaṃścaret puṇyadeśa saricchaiśaila vanāśramavatīṃ mahīm
translation: He should travel over the earth full of sacred lands, holy rivers, mountains, forests[10] and hermitages, entering cities, villages, cowherd settlements and caravansaries just for begging alms.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
EK. gives a suggestive list of holy places.

devanagari: वानप्रस्थाश्रमपदेषु अभीक्ष्णं भैक्ष्यमाचरेत् । संसिध्यत्याश्वसंमोहः शुद्धसत्त्वः शिलान्धसा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: vānaprasthāśramapadeṣu abhīkṣṇaṃ bhaikṣyamācaret saṃsidhyatyāśvasaṃmohaḥ śuddhasattvaḥ śilāndhasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [locative single from √ sidh class 4 verb]

translation: A Sannyāsin should frequently visit the hermitages of the Vānaprasthas. For (the food he gets is prepared out of foodgrains gleaned from cornfields and market places and) by such food from gleaned food-grains, his mind becomes quickly purified and free from delusion, and he attains liberation.

devanagari: नैतद् वस्तुतया पश्येद् दृश्यमानं विनश्यति । असक्तचित्तो विरमेद् इहामुत्र चिकीर्षितात् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: naitad vastutayā paśyed dṛśyamānaṃ vinaśyati asaktacitto viramed ihāmutra cikīrṣitāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: He should not look upon this (sweet food or other objects of pleasure) as real, for whatever is visible is bound to perish. With his mind detached to objects in this world or in the next, he should cease to work for gaining them (here or hereafter).

devanagari: यदेतदात्मनि जगन् मनोवाक्प्राणसंहतम् । सर्वं मायेति तर्केण स्वस्थः त्यक्त्वा न तत्स्मरेत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadetadātmani jagan manovākprāṇasaṃhatam sarvaṃ māyeti tarkeṇa svasthaḥ tyaktvā na tatsmaret

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: He should, by logical reasoning, realize that all this world and this body constituted of mind, speech and vital breath is nothing but Māyā (Illusion) superimposed on the Āttman. He should renounce it and stabilising himself in his blissful self, he should never remember the world (or saṃsāra).

devanagari: ज्ञाननिष्ठो विरक्तो वा मद्‍भक्तो वाऽनपेक्षकः । सलिङ्गान् आश्रमान् त्यक्त्वा चरेद् अविधिगोचरः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānaniṣṭho virakto vā mad‍bhakto vā saliṅgān āśramān tyaktvā cared avidhigocaraḥ
translation: My devotee who is established in spiritual knowledge, unattached to the world and does not long for anything (including Mokṣa) should renounce not only the duties (and attachment) pertaining to his āśrama but even its external distinguishing marks. He should go on in his life without being governed by the Śāstric rules in the matter—(Not that he should give up purity, bathing, etc. but he should not be a subject to Śāstric injunctions).

devanagari: बुधो बालकवत् क्रीडेत् कुशलो जडवच्चरेत् । वदेद् उन्मत्तवद् विद्वान् गोचर्यां नैगमश्चरेत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: budho bālakavat krīḍet kuśalo jaḍavaccaret vaded unmattavad vidvān gocaryāṃ naigamaścaret

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kav class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kav class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kav class 1 verb]

translation: Such a person, though full of wisdom, should behave like a child (with no sense of respect or disrespect). Though well-versed in a skill, he should assume dullness. Though deeply learned, he should show himself as insane (as he has to please nobody). Though master of Vedas (and faithful to their teaching) he should behave like an animal.

devanagari: वेदवादरतो न स्यात् न पाखण्डी न हैतुकः । शुष्कवादविवादे न कञ्चित् पक्षं समाश्रयेत् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vedavādarato na syāt na pākhaṇḍī na haitukaḥ śuṣkavādavivāde na kañcit pakṣaṃ samāśrayet
translation: He should not take interest in discussion about Vedic interpretation (whether pertaining to sacrifices or rules of conduct). Nor should he be a non-believer in the Vedas or one expert in Logical reasoning. He should not be a participant in fruitless discussion.

devanagari: नोद्विजेत जनाद् धीरो जनं चोद्वेजयेत् न तु । अतिवादान् तितिक्षेत नावमन्येत कञ्चन । देहमुद्दिश्य पशुवत् वैरं कुर्यान् न केनचित् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: nodvijeta janād dhīro janaṃ codvejayet na tu ativādān titikṣeta nāvamanyeta kañcana dehamuddiśya paśuvat vairaṃ kuryān na kenacit
translation: Full of wisdom and fortitude, he should not be- afraid of anybody. Nor should he frighten away the people.. He should forbear harsh words nor should he insult anyone. For the sake of his body, he should not entertain enmity with anyone.

devanagari: एक एव परो ह्यात्मा भूतेष्वात्मन्यवस्थितः । यथेदमुदपात्रेषु भूतान्येकात्मकानि च ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: eka eva paro hyātmā bhūteṣvātmanyavasthitaḥ yathedamudapātreṣu bhūtānyekātmakāni ca
translation: For the Supreme Soul is but one. He abides in all. living beings as well as in one’s own-self, just as the Moon (only one entity) is reflected in different vessels full of water. All beings even from the point of physical bodies are one, as they are made of the same gross elements. (Hence there is no propriety in cherishing enmity with anyone).

devanagari: अलब्ध्वा न विषीदेत कालेकालेऽशनं क्वचित् । लब्ध्वा न हृष्येद्धृतिमानुभयं दैवतं त्रितम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: alabdhvā na viṣīdeta kālekāle labdhvā na hṛṣyeddhṛtimānubhayaṃ daivataṃ tritam
translation: He should not become despondent, if sometimes he did not get alms; nor should he feel elated if he gets food in time. Full of fortitude, he should realize that both, these (getting alms or failing to get it) are in the hands of the Providence.

devanagari: आहारार्थं समीहेत युक्तं तत् प्राणधारणम् । तत्त्वं विमृश्यते तेन तद् विज्ञाय विमुच्यते ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: āhārārthaṃ samīheta yuktaṃ tat prāṇadhāraṇam tattvaṃ vimṛśyate tena tad vijñāya vimucyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: (This does not mean that one should not go out for alms). He should try to get alms for subsistence; for it is proper and essential to sustain life. It is by sustaining life that truth can be investigated. And it is by realization of the truth, that one gets Liberation from Saṃsāra.

devanagari: यदृच्छयोपपन्नान्नमद्यात् श्रेष्ठमुतापरम् । तथा वासः तथा शय्यां प्राप्तं प्राप्तं भजेन् मुनिः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yadṛcchayopapannānnamadyāt śreṣṭhamutāparam tathā vāsaḥ tathā śayyāṃ prāptaṃ prāptaṃ bhajen muniḥ
translation: A sage should accept whatever food comes to his lot by the will of the Providence—whether it be excellent or otherwise; similarly, he must accept whatever clothes and beds as may be offered to him.

devanagari: शौचमाचमनं स्नानं न तु चोदनया चरेत् । अन्यांश्च नियमान् ज्ञानी यथाहं लीलयेश्वरः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: śaucamācamanaṃ snānaṃ na tu codanayā caret anyāṃśca niyamān jñānī yathāhaṃ līlayeśvaraḥ
translation: Just as I carry on my activities as a sport, a man of spiritual wisdom should practise his habits of cleanliness, ācamana (sipping of water before taking food, etc.), bath, and not in compliance of the rule of the Dharmaśāstra. He should behave similarly in other matters.

devanagari: न हि तस्य विकल्पाख्या या च मद् वीक्षया हता । आ देहान्तात् क्वचित् ख्यातिः ततः संपद्यते मया ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi tasya vikalpākhyā yā ca mad vīkṣayā hatā ā dehāntāt kvacit khyātiḥ tataḥ saṃpadyate mayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: He has no sense of difference in him any longer. Whatever such sense there might have been, has disappeared since his realization of me. Occasionally such a sense of perception may appear till the fall of his body, but thereafter he merges in me.

devanagari: दुःखोदर्केषु कामेषु जातनिर्वेद आत्मवान् । अजिज्ञासित मद्धर्मो गुरुं मुनिमुपाव्रजेत् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: duḥkhodarkeṣu kāmeṣu jātanirveda ātmavān ajijñāsita maddharmo guruṃ munimupāvrajet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: A person who is self-controlled and has become disgusted with objects of pleasure which (he knows) end up in misery, but is not enlightened in duties leading to my realization, should approach a sage as his preceptor.

devanagari: तावत् परिचरेद् भक्तः श्रद्धावान् अनसूयकः । यावद् ब्रह्म विजानीयान् मामेव गुरुमादृतः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvat paricared bhaktaḥ śraddhāvān anasūyakaḥ yāvad brahma vijānīyān māmeva gurumādṛtaḥ
translation: Full of devout faith and with malice towards none, he should devotedly serve his teacher respecting him as if he is myself, till he realizes his Brahman.

devanagari: यस्त्वसंयत षड्वर्गः प्रचण्डेन्द्रिय सारथिः । ज्ञानवैराग्यरहितः त्रिदण्डमुपजीवति ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yastvasaṃyata ṣaḍvargaḥ pracaṇḍendriya sārathiḥ jñānavairāgyarahitaḥ tridaṇḍamupajīvati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rah class 10 verb]

translation: But he who has not controlled the group of six enemies (viz. six passions like lust, anger, avarice, etc. Or his five senses and the mind) and whose mind the controller of his senses, has been deeply attached to objects of pleasures, is devoid of spiritual knowledge and dispassion and only makes his livelihood by the triple staff (the symbol of being a Sannyāsin).

devanagari: सुरान् आत्मानमात्मस्थं निह्नुते मां च धर्महा । अविपक्वकषायोऽस्माद् अमुष्माच्च विहीयते ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: surān ātmānamātmasthaṃ nihnute māṃ ca dharmahā avipakvakaṣāyo
translation: Such a person is a violator of Dharma. He deceives gods (by denying them their sacrificial oblations which he would have offered as a house-holder), himself (by being deprived of household-comforts he would have got, had he not been a Sannyāsin), me who abide in him (as he fails to realize me). His sins are not completely consumed and he loses his world as well as the next.

devanagari: भिक्षोः धर्मः शमोऽहिंसा तप ईक्षा वनौकसः । गृहिणो भूतरक्षेज्या द्विजस्याचार्यसेवनम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhikṣoḥ dharmaḥ śamo gṛhiṇo bhūtarakṣejyā dvijasyācāryasevanam
translation: The essence of the righteous course of conduct of a recluse (Sannyāsin) is self-control and abstinence from violence; that of the Vānaprastha (the renouncer who stays in a forest) consists of performance of austerities (as prescribed for this stage of life in Śāstras) and (attainment of) spiritual knowledge that of a householder constitutes of the protection of beings, and performance of pañca-mahāyajñas (offering of oblations to gods and ancestors, offering of food to preceptors, guests etc.) while that or a twice-born (a boy who has taken a new spiritual birth by the investiture of the sacred thread and has becomes) Brahmacārin—a celibate student—is the service of his preceptor.

devanagari: ब्रह्मचर्यं तपः शौचं संतोषो भूतसौहृदम् । गृहस्थस्यापि ऋतौ गन्तुः सर्वेषां मदुपासनम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmacaryaṃ tapaḥ śaucaṃ saṃtoṣo bhūtasauhṛdam gṛhasthasyāpi ṛtau gantuḥ sarveṣāṃ madupāsanam
translation: Celibacy (i.e. abstinence from contacts with one’s wife on nights prohibited by Śāstras), penance, (mental and bodily) purity, contentment, friendliness to all creatures constitute the Dharma (the pious duties) of a householder—(Please note that a householder) who goes to his wife on the days (sanctioned by scriptures) is also a celibate person, while devotion to me is a sacred duty common to all (irrespective of their particular Āśrama).

devanagari: इति मां यः स्वधर्मेण भजन् नित्यमनन्यभाक् । सर्वभूतेषु मद्‍भावो मद्‍भक्तिं विन्दते दृढाम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti māṃ yaḥ svadharmeṇa bhajan nityamananyabhāk sarvabhūteṣu mad‍bhāvo mad‍bhaktiṃ vindate dṛḍhām
translation: A person who worships me by observing the pious duties (laid down for his particular Varṇa and Āśrama) as mentioned above without any expectation in return or (does not deviate to some other god or to some other purpose), and who sees me as abiding (as an antaryāmin) in all beings, attains to the (highest type of) Devotion[11] to me in a short while.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
EK.: This is the fourth stage of Devotion,‘The Supreme Bhakti’ as compared with which all the four types of Muktis (types of liberation) are quite valueless—EB. 18.324-25.

devanagari: भक्त्योद्धवानपायिन्या सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् । सर्वोत्पत्त्यप्ययं ब्रह्म कारणं मोपयाति सः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktyoddhavānapāyinyā sarvalokamaheśvaram sarvotpattyapyayaṃ brahma kāraṇaṃ mopayāti saḥ
translation: It is by such unswerving (life-long) devotion, O Uddhava, that the devotee attains to me, the Supreme Ruler of all worlds, the source of the creation and destruction of all, and hence the cause of everything, the Brahman[12] himself (or the creator of the Vedas).
purport:
Footnote [12]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: The presiding Deity in Vaikuṇṭha. The devotee attains the Mukti called Sāmīpya (vicinity to the Lord)
EK.: The devotee attains to the fourth Bhakti called the Supreme Devotion, full of the highest bliss in which God and the devotee become one.—EB. 18.325-332.

devanagari: इति स्वधर्मनिर्णिक्तसत्त्वो निर्ज्ञातमद्गतिः । ज्ञानविज्ञान सम्पन्नो न चिरात् समुपैति माम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti svadharmanirṇiktasattvo nirjñātamadgatiḥ jñānavijñāna sampanno na cirāt samupaiti mām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: A person whose heart is purified by observing the pious duties prescribed for his particular social class (Varṇa) and stage in life (Āśrama) and who has understood my nature attains both spiritual wisdom and my direct realization reaches me in a short time.

devanagari: वर्णाश्रमवतां धर्म एष आचार लक्षणः । स एव मद्‍भक्तियुतो निःश्रेयसकरः परः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: varṇāśramavatāṃ dharma eṣa ācāra lakṣaṇaḥ sa eva mad‍bhaktiyuto niḥśreyasakaraḥ paraḥ
translation: This is the righteous course of conduct of persons belonging to the four social classes (Varṇa) and their four stages of life (Āśrama). When this very course is reinforced by devotion to me, it becomes the supremely efficacious means of attaining the summum bonum (Mokṣa).

devanagari: एतत्तेऽभिहितं साधो भवान् पृच्छति यच्च माम् । यथा स्वधर्मसंयुक्तो भक्तो मां समियात् परम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: etatte yathā svadharmasaṃyukto bhakto māṃ samiyāt param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
I have thus explained to you, O good Uddhava, whatever you had enquired of me. It is in this way that my devotee, while following the path of righteous duties (specified for his particular Varṇa and Āśrama), attains to me who am the Supreme (Brahman).
[back to top]
